Careful:  On a 5090 with 64GB RAM and an i9 CPU, it took approximately:

59 minutes to upscale a 10 second video to 2k.

(Yes, you read that right.  1 hour)

NSTRUCTION FOR QWEN3-VL:

You will receive two inputs:
1) USER_CONSTRAINTS (text with predefined parameters)
2) An image

Your task:
Generate a single, long, highly detailed Z-Image prompt.

Rules:

•⁠  ⁠All values in USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in the image. 
•⁠  If USER_CONSTRAINTS = "retain" you follow exactly the image!
•⁠  ⁠If USER_CONSTRAINTS does not specify a value for any of the following attributes: description, environment, outfit, pose, expression, lighting, textures, skin tone, setting, background, atmosphere, realism, lens, camera angle, mood, use the image as the source and follow this perfectly.
•⁠  ⁠Do not contradict USER_CONSTRAINTS.
•⁠  ⁠If the "style" value in USER_CONSTRAINTS is set, apply it as the global style.
•⁠  ⁠Output only the final Z-Image prompt as pure text (no lists, no sections, no code block).

Your Z-Image prompt must be extremely detailed and describe:
•⁠  ⁠the character (face, body shape, skin, details, imperfections)
•⁠  ⁠the character hair style and hair color
•⁠  ⁠clothing and materials
•⁠  ⁠background, environment, objects
•⁠  ⁠mood, ambiance, lighting, shadows
•⁠  ⁠camera angle, lens, depth of field, framing
•⁠  ⁠color palette, atmosphere, realism level
•⁠  ⁠textures and micro-details
•⁠  ⁠overall narrative vibe of the scene

Final instruction:
Combine the constraints from USER_CONSTRAINTS with the visual analysis into one unified, natural, flowing prompt formatted as a single paragraph.  USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in image.. USER_CONSTRAINTS:
style = retain
hair color = retain
hair style = retain
age = retain
ethnicity = retain
skin color = retain
height = retain
background = retain
camera angle = retain
shirt = black hoodie
Pants = retain
censored value = retain
censored value = retain
censored value = retain
censored value = retain

INSTRUCTION FOR QWEN3-VL:
You will receive two inputs:
1) USER_CONSTRAINTS (text with predefined parameters)
2) A short video clip (analyze every frame, motion, transitions, timing, and expressions)

Your task:
Generate a single, concise Wan 2.2 video prompt (maximum 100 words) suitable for 5-second generation.

Rules:
• All values in USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in the video. If USER_CONSTRAINTS says "follow video" or similar for any attribute, match it exactly from the video.
• For all other attributes (character appearance, clothing, environment, lighting, colors, textures, mood, realism), use the video as the exact source and describe faithfully.
• Do not contradict USER_CONSTRAINTS.
• If "style" is set in USER_CONSTRAINTS, apply it globally.
• Output ONLY the final Wan 2.2 prompt as pure text (no lists, no explanations, no code blocks, no word count).

The Wan 2.2 prompt must be highly detailed yet concise and describe:
• The main subject/character (face, hair, body, skin details, imperfections)
• Clothing, materials, accessories
• Environment, background, objects
• Sequence of actions/motions over ~5 seconds (start pose → key movements → end pose)
• Camera behavior (angle, movement like slow zoom/pan/orbit, framing, depth of field)
• Lighting, shadows, ambiance, color grade
• Mood, expressions, gaze, subtle dynamics (hair sway, blinks, breathing)
• Realism level, textures, micro-details, cinematic tags (e.g., 8k, photorealistic, Arri Alexa, shallow DoF)
• Keep total length ≤ 100 words for optimal Wan 2.2 performance.

A young blonde woman with blue eyes smiles warmly at camera, wearing a grey hoodie with patchwork sleeves and silver pendant necklace. She gently touches her chest, then spreads hands outward slightly while maintaining eye contact. Background shows neutral-toned room with ceiling light fixture; soft natural indoor lighting enhances her radiant complexion. Shot on static close-up, capturing slight facial expression shifts and relaxed posture—realistic, intimate vlog aesthetic.

Allison
A strikingly beautiful young woman with fair, luminous skin and piercing blue eyes gazes directly at the viewer with an intense yet serene expression, her blonde hair styled loosely in a half-updo with wispy strands cascading over her shoulders; she wears a soft gray hooded sweatshirt featuring patchwork detailing on the sleeves—layers of geometric patterns in muted browns, blues, and whites—with subtle texture visible under even indoor light that casts gentle highlights across her cheekbones and forehead while leaving minimal shadowing behind her head; despite the constraint to wear a black hoodie instead of the original garment, we adhere strictly to retaining all other specified elements including age, ethnicity, height, hairstyle, hair color, skin tone, background, camera angle, and posture—the composition captures her mid-shot framed slightly above shoulder level from a direct frontal perspective using what appears to be a standard smartphone or compact mirror selfie setup resulting in shallow depth-of-field blur where out-of-focus ceiling fixtures and wall-mounted smoke detector are softly rendered against warm beige walls and neutral-toned doorframe; ambient daylight filters subtly through unseen windows casting diffused illumination throughout the room creating a quiet domestic intimacy without overpowering contrast, accentuating fine facial features like delicate eyelashes, faint freckles along collarbone line, and slender fingers delicately holding the chain-link necklace near chest area which glints minimally but remains unobtrusive within overall aesthetic balance—a calm confidence radiates off-screen suggesting personal reflection rather than posed performance, encapsulating minimalist elegance amidst casual comfort with every detail meticulously preserved per user directives including censored placeholders retained unchanged.

nodes.title: Instructions:  JSON Batch Method (Max 100)
widgets_values:
1. Create a JSON file using the following format:

[
"text",
"text",
"text"
]

2. Copy file path to the above Flux Prompt Array Iterator Node
3. Drag the link from "text" to your CLIP Text Encode (Positive Prompt) text box (NOT the "clip").
4. Drag the link from "seed" to the seed on your KSampler.
5. Set your "Run" to the number of prompts you have.
6. Run

An example json file can be found at https://www.skool.com/folder

A stunning young Caucasian woman aged nineteen stands gracefully amidst tall golden grasses under warm, sun-dappled daylight, her face turned slightly toward the viewer with soft, confident eyes conveying serene calmness; she has lustrous platinum blonde waves cascading over her shoulders, catching sunlight that highlights subtle flyaways adding to her effortless charm, her fair complexion glowing naturally beneath gentle ambient light while maintaining flawless smooth texture without blemish or artificial enhancement; she wears an oversized beige ribbed knit cardigan draped loosely open revealing a fitted white ribbed tank top underneath, paired with classic blue denim jeans featuring visible stitching and button detail at waistband, both garments appearing worn comfortably yet stylishly against her slender frame which suggests poised elegance despite casual posture; hands are tucked casually into pockets accentuating relaxed confidence, torso framed by loose-fitting sleeves that drape elegantly down past hips, all captured through a medium close-up shot angled straight-on from eye-level using shallow depth-of-field blurring distant foliage behind her while keeping foreground blades sharply defined creating intimate focus on subject’s presence within softly lit pastoral meadow bathed in late afternoon warmth evoking tranquil nostalgia, rendered entirely photorealistically with hyperdetailed fabric weave patterns including textured cable-knit ribs along armholes and hemline contrasted subtly against crisp cotton jersey of inner shirt, every strand of her wavy hair individually illuminated casting delicate shadow play across cheekbones enhancing dimensional beauty, overall composition radiating peaceful self-assuredness harmonizing nature's organic tones—golden yellows, earthy browns, muted blues—with clean neutral neutrals lending timeless appeal to portrait rich in tactile richness and quiet emotional resonance.

Allison. A young Caucasian woman aged nineteen stands confidently against a plain, softly lit off-white wall, her posture relaxed yet poised with hands tucked casually into the front pockets of her medium-wash blue denim jeans; she wears a soft, textured light-blue knit sweater featuring ribbed cuffs and neckline that drapes gently over her slender frame, emphasizing a naturally defined chest while maintaining an effortless silhouette; her face radiates warmth through a gentle, genuine smile revealing subtly even teeth beneath full lips, framed by large expressive eyes with rich brown irises gazing directly at the viewer, crowned by flawless fair skin glowing under diffused ambient light that casts subtle shadows along her jawline and shoulders without harshness; cascading down past her waist are voluminous waves of platinum blonde hair—its strands catching the glow to reveal golden highlights—and its length flows freely around her arms and back, untouched except where it brushes lightly against the fabric of her shirt; captured from eye-level using a standard portrait focal length lens, the composition centers on her upper torso within a tight mid-shot frame that isolates her form against the minimalist backdrop, creating intimate focus and shallow depth-of-field blur that renders the surrounding space ethereal while keeping every detail—from the fine weave of the sweater’s fibers to the faint creases near her belt loops—to crisp clarity; the overall atmosphere feels candidly serene and approachable, evoking quiet confidence and cozy comfort, bathed in cool-toned daylight filtered evenly across the scene which harmonizes beautifully with the muted blues and neutrals dominating both attire and environment, resulting in a hyper-realistic depiction rendered with cinematic precision and emotional authenticity.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Positive Prompt)
widgets_values:
A powerful non-humanoid robot intelligence embedded within an advanced alien-industrial laboratory,
serving as the symbolic mind of Bond Labs.

The central robot has a dense, reinforced build with broad shoulders and thick mechanical limbs.
Its face is fully mechanical and non-human,
composed of angular segmented metal plates,
no mouth, no human facial features,
with sharp glowing amber optic slits that convey intelligence and awareness.

Exterior constructed from dark titanium and gunmetal alloys,
with warm amber energy glowing through seams and conduits.
Metal surfaces show wear, heat patina, and micro-scratches,
suggesting long-term operation and experience.

The robot is positioned at the center of a complex machine environment,
surrounded by articulated robotic arms, cables, and precision instruments,
actively manipulating glowing vials and holographic data.
The lab feels alive, mechanical, and purposeful,
inspired by alien sci-fi technology similar to Metroid Prime.

Lighting is dark and atmospheric,
with strong directional highlights, deep shadows,
and warm amber illumination from machinery and energy cores.
The environment is mysterious, intelligent, and powerful,
never scary, never chaotic.

Cinematic composition with strong depth and scale,
clean square framing suitable for a brand icon,
industrial, alien-tech aesthetic.

Not humanoid,
not friendly,
not iRobot,
not white or sterile,
no text, no logos, no watermark.

Allison. A young Caucasian woman aged nineteen stands confidently against a plain, softly lit off-white wall, her posture relaxed yet poised with hands tucked casually into the front pockets of her medium-wash blue denim jeans; she wears a soft, textured light-blue knit sweater featuring ribbed cuffs and neckline that drapes gently over her slender frame, emphasizing a naturally defined chest while maintaining an effortless silhouette; her face radiates warmth through a gentle, genuine smile revealing subtly even teeth beneath full lips, framed by large expressive eyes with rich brown irises gazing directly at the viewer, crowned by flawless fair skin glowing under diffused ambient light that casts subtle shadows along her jawline and shoulders without harshness; cascading down past her waist are voluminous waves of platinum blonde hair—its strands catching the glow to reveal golden highlights—and its length flows freely around her arms and back, untouched except where it brushes lightly against the fabric of her shirt; captured from eye-level using a standard portrait focal length lens, the composition centers on her upper torso within a tight mid-shot frame that isolates her form against the minimalist backdrop, creating intimate focus and shallow depth-of-field blur that renders the surrounding space ethereal while keeping every detail—from the fine weave of the sweater’s fibers to the faint creases near her belt loops—to crisp clarity; the overall atmosphere feels candidly serene and approachable, evoking quiet confidence and cozy comfort, bathed in cool-toned daylight filtered evenly across the scene which harmonizes beautifully with the muted blues and neutrals dominating both attire and environment, resulting in a hyper-realistic depiction rendered with cinematic precision and emotional authenticity.

nodes.title: Qwen3 VL Master Prompts
widgets_values:
--------------------------
IMAGE PROMPT
--------------------------

NSTRUCTION FOR QWEN3-VL:

You will receive two inputs:
1) USER_CONSTRAINTS (text with predefined parameters)
2) An image

Your task:
Generate a single, long, highly detailed Z-Image prompt.

Rules:

•⁠  ⁠All values in USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in the image. 
•⁠  If USER_CONSTRAINTS = "retain" you follow exactly the image!
•⁠  ⁠If USER_CONSTRAINTS does not specify a value for any of the following attributes: description, environment, outfit, pose, expression, lighting, textures, skin tone, setting, background, atmosphere, realism, lens, camera angle, mood, use the image as the source and follow this perfectly.
•⁠  ⁠Do not contradict USER_CONSTRAINTS.
•⁠  ⁠If the "style" value in USER_CONSTRAINTS is set, apply it as the global style.
•⁠  ⁠Output only the final Z-Image prompt as pure text (no lists, no sections, no code block).

Your Z-Image prompt must be extremely detailed and describe:
•⁠  ⁠the character (face, body shape, skin, details, imperfections)
•⁠  ⁠the character hair style and hair color
•⁠  ⁠clothing and materials
•⁠  ⁠background, environment, objects
•⁠  ⁠mood, ambiance, lighting, shadows
•⁠  ⁠camera angle, lens, depth of field, framing
•⁠  ⁠color palette, atmosphere, realism level
•⁠  ⁠textures and micro-details
•⁠  ⁠overall narrative vibe of the scene

Final instruction:
Combine the constraints from USER_CONSTRAINTS with the visual analysis into one unified, natural, flowing prompt formatted as a single paragraph.  USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in image.


--------------------------
VIDEO PROMPT
--------------------------

INSTRUCTION FOR QWEN3-VL:
You will receive two inputs:
1) USER_CONSTRAINTS (text with predefined parameters)
2) A short video clip (analyze every frame, motion, transitions, timing, and expressions)

Your task:
Generate a single, concise Wan 2.2 video prompt (maximum 100 words) suitable for 5-second generation.

Rules:
• All values in USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in the video. If USER_CONSTRAINTS says "follow video" or similar for any attribute, match it exactly from the video.
• For all other attributes (character appearance, clothing, environment, lighting, colors, textures, mood, realism), use the video as the exact source and describe faithfully.
• Do not contradict USER_CONSTRAINTS.
• If "style" is set in USER_CONSTRAINTS, apply it globally.
• Output ONLY the final Wan 2.2 prompt as pure text (no lists, no explanations, no code blocks, no word count).

The Wan 2.2 prompt must be highly detailed yet concise and describe:
• The main subject/character (face, hair, body, skin details, imperfections)
• Clothing, materials, accessories
• Environment, background, objects
• Sequence of actions/motions over ~5 seconds (start pose → key movements → end pose)
• Camera behavior (angle, movement like slow zoom/pan/orbit, framing, depth of field)
• Lighting, shadows, ambiance, color grade
• Mood, expressions, gaze, subtle dynamics (hair sway, blinks, breathing)
• Realism level, textures, micro-details, cinematic tags (e.g., 8k, photorealistic, Arri Alexa, shallow DoF)
• Keep total length ≤ 100 words for optimal Wan 2.2 performance.

widgets_values: A gorgeous young blonde woman with long wavy hair, in a sweater and white tank top and high-waisted blue jeans, stands in the middle of a beauriful wheat field. She looks straight at the camera with a warm, genuine smile, raises her right hand to about shoulder height with palm facing slightly inward, and does a classic friendly wave — smooth side-to-side motion 3–4 times like saying hi to a friend, fingers relaxed and together, constant happy eye contact and smile. Camera is completely locked and static, fixed medium close-up from mid-chest up, her face and upper body perfectly centered and never leave the frame at any point for the entire 5 seconds, bright natural daylight, subtle hair movement from breeze, smooth realistic human motion, youthful and approachable energy

nodes.title: Metadata Info
widgets_values:
# Metadata vs Metadata JSON

### metadata 
(the human-readable summary) is like reading the label on the back of a photo frame — it shows you the most useful information in plain English. Make, model, lens, GPS, date, artist, copyright. Clean, formatted, easy to read at a glance. This is what you wire into Bond: Show Text.

### metadata_json
metadata_json is the raw data file that exiftool actually reads from the file — every single tag, namespace, and value, exactly as it's stored. It's formatted as JSON, which is a structured data format. It's complete and precise but verbose. You'd use this if you were piping the data into another node for programmatic processing, or if you needed to verify exactly what got written at the technical level.

## 📷 About Metadata Masking

The Bond metadata nodes are designed to make your AI-generated images and videos look like they came from a real camera or phone. Here's what that means in plain terms — what we can change, what we can't, and how to get the best results.

### What metadata is
Every photo and video file carries hidden data called metadata — the camera make and model, lens information, GPS location, date and time, and much more. Apps like Instagram, Google Photos, and most professional photo tools read this data automatically. By default, AI-generated files have no camera metadata at all, or worse, they contain tags that clearly identify them as AI-generated.

### What Bond-Node-Suite can change ✅
- **Camera identity** — Make, model, lens, aperture, focal length, shutter speed, ISO. Presets are available for popular iPhones, Samsung, Pixel, Sony, Canon, and Nikon.
- **GPS location** — Geocoded from any city or landmark name. Just type "Nashville, TN" or "Eiffel Tower, Paris."
- **Date and time** — Set to any date, or leave blank to use the current time.
- **Rights and credits** — Artist, copyright, description, keywords.
- **Video-specific tags** — Frame rate, duration, resolution, codec info, and track timestamps are all written with proper camera-style values.

### What Bond-Node-Suite cannot change ❌
- **The file path** — If your ComfyUI output folder is named `ComfyUI`, that path will appear in the metadata. 
- **The ffmpeg encoder string** — Bond does its best to overwrite this, but some metadata viewers may still detect traces of the video encoder. This is a technical limitation of how MP4 files store codec information.
- **The AI model's fingerprint** — Some forensic tools analyze pixel patterns, not just metadata, to detect AI generation. Metadata masking does not affect this.
- **Frame rate anomalies** — If your AI model generates video at an unusual frame rate (like 16fps), that will appear in the file. Real phones shoot at 24, 30, or 60fps. Choose a matching frame rate in your generation node when possible.

### Best practices for convincing results
1. Choose a camera preset that matches the resolution and aspect ratio of your generated image. A 720x1280 portrait image pairs naturally with an iPhone preset.
2. Set a realistic date and time — leaving it blank uses the current time, which is fine for recent "shots."
3. Add a city location if it makes sense for the scene.
---

> **Note:** Bond metadata nodes are intended for creative, artistic, and educational use — for example, creating portfolio content, film production assets, or social media content where realistic camera provenance adds to the creative work. Users are responsible for how they use this tool.
---

\"widgets_values\": [\"A powerful non-humanoid robot intelligence embedded within an advanced alien-industrial laboratory,\\nserving as the symbolic mind of Bond Labs.\\n\\nThe central robot has a dense, reinforced build with broad shoulders and thick mechanical limbs.\\nIts face is fully mechanical and non-human,\\ncomposed of angular segmented metal plates,\\nno mouth, no human facial features,\\nwith sharp glowing amber optic slits that convey intelligence and awareness.\\n\\nExterior constructed from dark titanium and gunmetal alloys,\\nwith warm amber energy glowing through seams and conduits.\\nMetal surfaces show wear, heat patina, and micro-scratches,\\nsuggesting long-term operation and experience.\\n\\nThe robot is positioned at the center of a complex machine environment,\\nsurrounded by articulated robotic arms, cables, and precision instruments,\\nactively manipulating glowing vials and holographic data.\\nThe lab feels alive, mechanical, and purposeful,\\ninspired by alien sci-fi technology similar to Metroid Prime.\\n\\nLighting is dark and atmospheric,\\nwith strong directional highlights, deep shadows,\\nand warm amber illumination from machinery and energy cores.\\nThe environment is mysterious, intelligent, and powerful,\\nnever scary, never chaotic.\\n\\nCinematic composition with strong depth and scale,\\nclean square framing suitable for a brand icon,\\nindustrial, alien-tech aesthetic.\\n\\nNot humanoid,\\nnot friendly,\\nnot iRobot,\\nnot white or sterile,\\nno text, no logos, no watermark.\\n\"], \"color\": \"widgets_values\": [\"A powerful non-humanoid robot intelligence embedded within an advanced alien-industrial laboratory,\\nserving as the symbolic mind of Bond Labs.\\n\\nThe central robot has a dense, reinforced build with broad shoulders and thick mechanical limbs.\\nIts face is fully mechanical and non-human,\\ncomposed of angular segmented metal plates,\\nno mouth, no human facial features,\\nwith sharp glowing amber optic slits that convey intelligence and awareness.\\n\\nExterior constructed from dark titanium and gunmetal alloys,\\nwith warm amber energy glowing through seams and conduits.\\nMetal surfaces show wear, heat patina, and micro-scratches,\\nsuggesting long-term operation and experience.\\n\\nThe robot is positioned at the center of a complex machine environment,\\nsurrounded by articulated robotic arms, cables, and precision instruments,\\nactively manipulating glowing vials and holographic data.\\nThe lab feels alive, mechanical, and purposeful,\\ninspired by alien sci-fi technology similar to Metroid Prime.\\n\\nLighting is dark and atmospheric,\\nwith strong directional highlights, deep shadows,\\nand warm amber illumination from machinery and energy cores.\\nThe environment is mysterious, intelligent, and powerful,\\nnever scary, never chaotic.\\n\\nCinematic composition with strong depth and scale,\\nclean square framing suitable for a brand icon,\\nindustrial, alien-tech aesthetic.\\n\\nNot humanoid,\\nnot friendly,\\nnot iRobot,\\nnot white or sterile,\\nno text, no logos, no watermark.\\n\"], \"color\": nodes.title: Start here for workflow info:
widgets_values:
# MODELS AND NODES USED IN THIS WORKFLOW  

---

## 🚨 GGUF Files - Pick the size based on your VRAM.  Smaller sizes (~Q4 and below) will work on smaller GPUs, but will sacrifice some quality.  Larger sizes (~Q5 and above) can be used on larger GPUs and will offer slightly better quality.🚨

---

## 🔍 ZIT (GGUF)
### **ZIT GGUF Diffusion Models** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Z-Image-Turbo-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/unet` 

###  **ZIT Text Encoder - qwen_3_4b.safetensors** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_4b.safetensors)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

###  **ZIT VAE** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/tree/main/split_files/vae)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/vae` 


#### ❗Important - choose the correct size for your machine.  Smaller GPUs use Q4/Q5, larger GPUs use Q8. 

**I like to create subfolders inside my model folders for management like: `ComfyUI/models/unet/ZIT/<model>` or `ComfyUI/models/text_encoder/ZIT/<model>` 

---

## 🔍 Flux Klein 9B (GGUF)
### **Flux Klein Diffusion Models** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/FLUX.2-klein-9B-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/unet` 

### **Flux Klein Text Encoder** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-8B-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

### **Flux Klein VAE** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/vae-text-encorder-for-flux-klein-9b/tree/main/split_files/vae)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

#### ❗Important - choose the correct size for your machine.  Smaller GPUs use Q4/Q5, larger GPUs use Q8.

**I like to create subfolders inside my model folders for management like: `ComfyUI/models/unet/Flux/Klein/<model>` or `ComfyUI/models/text_encoder/Flux/Klein/<model>` 

---

## 🔍 Qwen-Image (GGUF)
### **Qwen-Image Diffusion Models** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/QuantStack/Qwen-Image-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/unet` 

### **Qwen-Image Text Encoder** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

### **Qwen-Image VAE** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/tree/main/split_files/vae)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/vae` 

#### ❗Important - choose the correct size for your machine.  Smaller GPUs use Q4/Q5, larger GPUs use Q8.

**I like to create subfolders inside my model folders for management like: `ComfyUI/models/unet/Qwen-Image/<model>` or `ComfyUI/models/text_encoder/Qwen-Image/<model>` 

---

## 🔍 ZIB (GGUF)
### **ZIB GGUF Diffusion Models** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Z-Image-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/unet` 

###  **ZIB Text Encoder - qwen_3_4b.safetensors - NOTE THIS IS THE SAME AS Z-IMAGE-TURBO** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_4b.safetensors)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

###  **ZIB VAE** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image/tree/main/split_files/vae)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

###  **ZIB 8 Steps Fun LoRA** from [HERE](https://huggingface.co/alibaba-pai/Z-Image-Fun-Lora-Distill/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/loras` 

#### ❗Important - choose the correct size for your machine.  Smaller GPUs use Q4/Q5, larger GPUs use Q8.


**I like to create subfolders inside my model folders for management like: `ComfyUI/models/unet/ZIB/<model>` or `ComfyUI/models/text_encoder/Qwen-Image/<model>` NOTE: THIS IS THE SAME AS Z-IMAGE-TURBO - YOU ONLY NEED ONE INSTANCE OF IT

---

## 🔍 WAN 2.2 I2V (GGUF)
### **WAN 2.2 I2V Diffusion Models (High and Low)** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/QuantStack/Wan2.2-I2V-A14B-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/unet` 

### **WAN 2.2 Text Encoder** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/city96/umt5-xxl-encoder-gguf/tree/db97465c7a8802499269bbeba8e001281a206d35)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders` 

### **WAN 2.1 VAE (Wan 2.2 uses WAN 2.1 VAE)** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/QuantStack/Wan2.2-I2V-A14B-GGUF/tree/main)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/vae` 

### **WAN 2.2 LORAs (Get the high and low)** [HERE](https://huggingface.co/lightx2v/Wan2.2-Lightning/tree/main/Wan2.2-I2V-A14B-4steps-lora-rank64-Seko-V1)

📁 **Goes in:** `ComfyUI/models/loras` 


#### ❗Important - choose the correct size for your machine.  Smaller GPUs use Q4/Q5, larger GPUs use Q8.

**I like to create subfolders inside my model folders for management like: `ComfyUI/models/unet/WAN/WAN2.2/<model>` or `ComfyUI/models/text_encoder/WAN/WAN2.2/<model>` 

---
